headline: A $2 Million Dividend Portfolio Cut Its Distribution by $14,400 in One Year and the Holders Did Not Sell
summary: A retired couple with a $2 million dividend-focused portfolio yielding roughly 6%, or $120,000 per year, watched their income stream fall by $14,400 during a difficult market period as covered-call funds, mortgage REITs, and business development companies trimmed distributions. Their annual income dropped to $105,600. They did not sell a share. Understanding why they were ... A $2 Million Dividend Portfolio Cut Its Distribution by $14,400 in One Year and the Holders Did Not Sell

headline: IMAAVY® (nipocalimab-aahu) demonstrates durable hemoglobin response and rapid onset of effect in pivotal Phase 2/3 study in warm autoimmune hemolytic anemia (wAIHA), an autoantibody-driven disease with no FDA-approved therapies
summary: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) today is presenting the first comprehensive results from the Phase 2/3 ENERGY study showing that IMAAVY® (nipocalimab-aahu) produced a statistically significant durable hemoglobin (Hgb) responseb with rapid onset of effect in patients with warm autoimmune hemolytic anemia (wAIHA)e in the 30 mg/kg treatment group,a compared with those who received placebo. The randomized, placebo-controlled trial demonstrated approximately three times as many patients achieved durabl

headline: Johnson & Johnson Reports Strong Late-Stage Results for Rare Blood Disorder Therapy (JNJ)
summary: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) has announced positive findings from its Phase 2/3 ENERGY clinical trial, with IMAAVY (nipocalimab-aahu) demonstrating statistically significant improvements in hemoglobin levels among patients with warm autoimmune hemolytic anemia compared with placebo. The results provide further support for the therapy as the company seeks regulatory approval for a condition that currently has no FDA-approved treatment options.

content.title: Johnson & Johnson Reports Strong Late-Stage Results for Rare Blood Disorder Therapy (JNJ)
content.summary: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) has announced positive findings from its Phase 2/3 ENERGY clinical trial, with IMAAVY (nipocalimab-aahu) demonstrating statistically significant improvements in hemoglobin levels among patients with warm autoimmune hemolytic anemia compared with placebo. The results provide further support for the therapy as the company seeks regulatory approval for a condition that currently has no FDA-approved treatment options.

headline: Johnson & Johnson Expands U.S. Availability of TECNIS PureSee IOL, an Advanced Lens Option for Cataract Surgeons and Patients
summary: JACKSONVILLE, Fla., June 11, 2026--Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) is expanding the U.S. roll-out of its latest advancement in presbyopia-correcting intraocular lenses (PC-IOLs). TECNIS PureSee IOL is an extended depth of focus (EDOF) lens designed to support visual clarity and quality for patients undergoing cataract surgery.3

content.title: Johnson & Johnson Expands U.S. Availability of TECNIS PureSee IOL, an Advanced Lens Option for Cataract Surgeons and Patients
content.summary: JACKSONVILLE, Fla., June 11, 2026--Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) is expanding the U.S. roll-out of its latest advancement in presbyopia-correcting intraocular lenses (PC-IOLs). TECNIS PureSee IOL is an extended depth of focus (EDOF) lens designed to support visual clarity and quality for patients undergoing cataract surgery.3

headline: Assessing Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum
summary: Event overview and recent stock performance Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) has drawn fresh attention after recent share price moves, with the stock up about 7.7% over the past month and roughly 15% year to date. See our latest analysis for Johnson & Johnson. The recent 7.7% 30 day share price return, alongside a 15.0% year to date share price return and a 1 year total shareholder return of 57.4%, points to momentum building as investors reassess Johnson & Johnson’s long term prospects and risk...
